data.title: Investors in CPNG stock from April 6, 2025, to Dec 16, 2025, can join a class action lawsuit. Contact Robbins LLP.
data.description: In San Diego on January 21, 2026, Robbins LLP issued a reminder to stockholders regarding a class action lawsuit filed for investors who bought or acquired securities of Coupang, Inc. (NYSE: CPNG) between April 6, 2025, and December 16, 2025. Coupang, a company claiming to be a prominent player in the industry, faced legal action due to alleged misconduct during the specified period. The lawsuit encompasses all individuals involved in trading CPNG stocks during this eight-month timeframe.

data.title: Correction made by Carnegie Foundation and ETS regarding Skills Progressions for Collaboration, Communication, and Critical Thinking released on 21-Jan-2026.
data.description: The Carnegie Foundation for the Advancement of Teaching and ETS recently unveiled the initial Skills Progressions for Collaboration, Communication, and Critical Thinking on January 21, 2026. This release marks the first of its kind in partnership with ETS. The progressions provide a framework for assessing and developing key skills essential for success in the modern world. This significant announcement was made via a press release distributed over PR Newswire. This collaboration aims to enhance proficiency in important areas such as collaboration, communication, and critical thinking, setting a precedent for future skill development initiatives.

data.title: Investors who lost money in F5, Inc. (NASDAQ: FFIV) should contact Robbins LLP for info on their rights in class action lawsuit.
data.description: On January 21, 2026, Robbins LLP issued a reminder to stockholders regarding a class action lawsuit for investors who bought or acquired F5, Inc. (NASDAQ: FFIV) securities between October 28, 2024, and October 27, 2025. F5 is a global multicloud application company.

data.title: Is Microsoft's AI Spending Paying Off?
data.description: Microsoft Corp. reported solid earnings, easing investor concerns about the return on its heavy investment in artificial intelligence infrastructure. Azure growth remained strong, reassuring investors despite a slight moderation in growth rate. The market is now focused on Microsoft's ability to efficiently convert its AI lead into earnings power, rather than just winning the AI race. Azure growth is seen as a cash generating platform, with more emphasis on margin contribution over headline growth rates. Microsoft's capital expenditures for AI infrastructure, including data center expansion and specialized chips, continue to raise questions about margins. Despite this, the market appears willing to accept a more mature growth profile as long as Azure maintains steady growth.

data.title: Call for entries for 19th annual North American Copper in Architecture Awards by CDA, now accepting submissions for 2026.
data.description: The Copper Development Association (CDA), a non-profit organization representing the global copper industry and based in McLean, Virginia, has announced that they are currently open to receiving entries for the 2026 competition. Architects, designers, contractors, and various other professionals in the construction industry are encouraged to submit their proposals. The submissions for this year can be sent to the CDA, and the deadline for entries is anticipated to be in the near future. This competition provides a platform for individuals and teams to showcase their innovative ideas and projects related to copper, with the chance to win recognition and rewards for their work.

data.title: Bitcoin hits $89,772, volatile day with highs at $90,000. 179,931 traders liquidated in 24 hours for $807.30M. News on Benzinga.com.
data.description: Bitcoin has surged back above $89,000 after experiencing a volatile trading day where it reached both $88,000 and $90,000. The current cryptocurrency prices are as follows: Bitcoin at $89,772, Ethereum at $3,010, Solana at $130.39, XRP at $1.96, Dogecoin at $0.1269, and Shiba Inu at $0.057969. Coinglass data reveals that 179,931 traders were liquidated in the last 24 hours, resulting in losses totaling $807.30 million. Among the top gainers in the past 24 hours are Canton, MYX Finance, and LayerZero. Ripple's President predicts that half of the Fortune 500 companies will have crypto strategies by year-end. Additionally, Bitcoin saw an 11% decrease after Trump's first year. For further details, refer to the full story on Benzinga.com.

data.title: US Crypto czar predicts banks and crypto will blend as one industry, addressing issues like stablecoin yield and stalled legislation.
data.description: During a recent interview at the World Economic Forum in Davos, David Sacks discussed the controversies surrounding stablecoin yield and the current stalling of legislation regarding the structure of the crypto market in the US Senate. The discussions, which took place amidst a backdrop of regulatory scrutiny and uncertainty, shed light on key issues facing the cryptocurrency industry. David Sacks, a prominent figure in the sector, highlighted the importance of addressing these issues given the growing market capitalization of stablecoins, which currently stands at around $120 billion. Additionally, he emphasized the need for clearer regulations to instill confidence in investors and ensure the long-term stability and growth of the crypto market. The interview underscored the complexities and challenges faced by the industry as it navigates evolving regulatory landscapes and strives for mainstream acceptance.

data.title: Key points from Supreme Court's hearing on Trump firing Lisa Cook: Justices cautious of rushed decisions, emphasize central bank's importance.
data.description: The members of the judiciary were conscious of the significant role played by the central bank and cautious about making a sweeping decision due to limited briefing and insufficient data available.

data.title: Info-Tech Research Group's playbook outlines 12 steps to scale AI effectively. Many struggle with fragmented strategies and governance.
data.description: The majority of organizations, despite experiencing significant growth in AI testing, struggle to effectively implement AI on a large scale or showcase long-term business benefits. According to data from Info-Tech Research Group, issues such as fractured strategies, inadequate governance, and a lack of execution discipline are hindering progress.

data.title: CME Group breaks record with 2,576,346 natural gas contracts traded on January 20, up 15% from previous high of 2,239,081.
data.description: On January 20, 2026, CME Group, the top derivatives marketplace worldwide, revealed that their natural gas complex set a new single-day peak with 2,576,346 contracts traded, marking a 15% increase from the earlier record of 2,239,081 contracts traded. This announcement was made in Chicago on January 21, 2026.

data.title: Dow Jones rises on Trump's Davos speech; Netflix stocks plummet on earnings. S&P 500 higher; Johnson & Johnson slides.
data.description: On Wednesday, the Dow Jones index experienced an increase as President Trump addressed the Davos summit. In contrast, Netflix's stock took a significant hit following their earnings report. The latest stock market updates reveal that the S&P 500 saw a rise after the news from Davos, while Johnson & Johnson faced a decline. This information was provided by Investor's Business Daily.

data.title: NABIP backs FY 2026 HHS funding package health provisions, pushes for action by Jan 30 deadline.
data.description: On January 21, 2026, the National Association of Benefits and Insurance Professionals (NABIP) publicly endorsed various healthcare measures within the current government funding plan. These provisions, which resonate with NABIP's established stance, have garnered significant backing from the organization. The NABIP's endorsement underscores the importance of key healthcare initiatives being considered in the latest funding package.
